title: Monster Beverage (MNST)Intrinsic Value
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on Monster Beverage using FY2025 actuals. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what MNST is really worth.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale:
">
Revenue grew consistently from $5.5B in 2021 to $8.3B in 2025. A 12% FCF growth assumption models continued international expansion, product portfolio diversification, and robust pricing power, while acknowledging scale makes high double-digit growth harder.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
">
Revenue grew consistently from $5.5B in 2021 to $8.3B in 2025. A 12% FCF growth assumption models continued international expansion, product portfolio diversification, and robust pricing power, while acknowledging scale makes high double-digit growth harder.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
">
Revenue grew consistently from $5.5B in 2021 to $8.3B in 2025. A 12% FCF growth assumption models continued international expansion, product portfolio diversification, and robust pricing power, while acknowledging scale makes high double-digit growth harder.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why use DCF instead of a P/E or EV/EBITDA multiple?
faq.answer: Westmount Research. "Monster Beverage (MNST) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, March 18, 2026.
